topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The printout lay spread across the kitchen table, the ink stark against the white paper. My hands were shaking so badly I had to grip the edge of the wood to steady myself. I reread the section labeled 'Biological Match' for the hundredth time, my breath catching in shallow gasps. It wasn't just a probability; it was a confirmation. My eyes scanned the names listed under 'Genetic Origin,' and a profound stillness settled over me, a quiet shock that made the air feel thick. I slowly lowered the paper, the movement deliberate, as if handling something fragile and immensely valuable. A strange lightness bloomed in my chest, chasing away a persistent knot of uncertainty that had been living in my stomach for years. I looked toward the window, the sunlight suddenly seeming impossibly bright, and whispered a single, disbelieving word.

[story 2]

When the results finally appeared on the screen, Leo just stared. His fingers hovered over the mouse, unable to move, his jaw tight. The numbers swam before his eyes—the shared markers, the undeniable lineage. He leaned back in his chair, the worn fabric groaning under his weight, and let out a slow, shaky exhale. He pressed his palms flat against his temples, feeling the sudden, dizzying shift in his entire world. It was as if the foundation of his existence had just been revealed, solid and undeniably true. He pushed himself up from the desk, walking to the nearest bookshelf, running his fingers over the spines of books he’d never dared to touch before. A slow, profound smile began to spread across his face, a genuine, unrestrained expression that made his eyes crinkle at the corners.

[story 3]

I kept touching the confirmation email, tracing the lines of the genetic report with my fingertip. Every time I looked at the DNA profile, a wave of something overwhelming washed over me—a dizzying sense of recognition, like remembering a melody I’d forgotten. My gaze drifted from the screen to my own reflection in the dark glass of the monitor. There was a new depth in my eyes, a startling clarity. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small confines of my room, my movements quick and uncertain. A low sound escaped my throat, half-laugh, half-sob, as the sheer scope of what I had just learned settled deep into my bones, rearranging everything I thought I knew about myself.
